Guideline: Use FlatList instead of ScrollView for long lists
Description: ScrollView renders all items upfront causing memory issues and slow initial render with 50+ items
Do: Use FlatList or SectionList; provide keyExtractor; set initialNumToRender=10; windowSize=5 for large lists
Don't: Use ScrollView.children=[...1000items]; render all items unconditionally

Description: Every parent re-render re-creates child components in list; without memo rows re-render even when their data hasn't changed
Do: Wrap row component in React.memo(); use useCallback for callbacks; avoid passing new object references as props on each render
Don't: Pass inline arrow functions as event handlers directly from parent to list item without memoization

Guideline: Avoid complex computations in render path
Description: Expensive calculations in component body or render function run on every re-render degrading animation and UI smoothness
Do: Use useMemo for expensive derived data; move static data outside component; useCallback for handlers; avoid creating Date/Objects inline in render
Don't: Filtering/sorting large arrays inline in JSX; creating new object literals as style props on every render

Guideline: React Navigation Stack: configure screen options correctly
Description: Screen options misconfiguration causes visual glitches incorrect headers and broken transitions
Do: Set screenOptions on navigator for defaults; per-screen options.title; headerBackTitle for iOS back label; headerShown:false for screens with custom header
Don't: Mixing navigation v5 and v6 APIs; hardcoding screen dimensions for navigation bar height

Guideline: Use Linking API for deep links
Description: Deep links require platform configuration plus React Navigation Linking setup for URL-to-screen routing
Do: Define linkingConfig with prefixes and screens map; test with adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d yourapp://path; test iOS with xcrun simctl openurl
Don't: Manually parse URL strings in useEffect; use navigation.navigate without link structure
Code Good: const linking = { prefixes: ['myapp://'], screens: { Home: 'home', Profile: 'profile/:userId' } }; <NavigationContainer linking={linking}>
Code Bad: useEffect(()=>{ const url = Linking.getInitialURL(); navigate based on custom URL parser })

Description: The built-in SafeAreaView from RN core is unreliable and doesn't handle all device notch variants; always use the context version
Do: Install react-native-safe-area-context; wrap app in SafeAreaProvider; use useSafeAreaInsets() or SafeAreaView
Don't: Use import {SafeAreaView} from 'react-native' (unreliable); hardcode padding values for notch
Code Good: import { SafeAreaView, useSafeAreaInsets } from 'react-native-safe-area-context'; const {top} = useSafeAreaInsets();

Description: StyleSheet.create validates style fields and optimizes style objects by ID rather than object reference on each render
Do: Define all styles in StyleSheet.create at module level; reference by name; combine with array style prop for conditional styles
Don't: Define styles as plain objects or inline in JSX; use dynamic StyleSheet.create inside render function
Code Good: const styles = StyleSheet.create({ container: { flex: 1, padding: 16, backgroundColor: '#fff' } })
Code Bad: const styles = { container: { flex: 1, padding: 16 } }; // No StyleSheet.create

Description: Text inputs behind the keyboard need the view to adjust; wrong behavior prop causes incorrect or no adjustment
Do: Use behavior='padding' on iOS; behavior='height' on Android; wrap only the screen portion below the nav bar
Don't: Wrap entire screen including navigation bar; use fixed bottom padding instead; forget to set behavior prop

Description: iOS and Android have different visual conventions; forcing one style on both looks wrong or broken
Do: Use Platform.select() or Platform.OS===ios check for elevation vs shadow; font weights; subtle color differences
Don't: Use identical styles everywhere ignoring platform visual conventions; use Platform.OS inside render on hot path
Code Good: const shadowStyle = Platform.select({ ios: { shadowColor:'#000', shadowOffset:{width:0,height:2}, shadowOpacity:0.1, shadowRadius:4 }, android: { elevation: 4 } })
Code Bad: const shadowStyle = { shadowColor:'#000', shadowOffset:{width:0,height:2}, shadowOpacity:0.1, elevation:4 } // mixed won't work on Android

Guideline: Add accessibilityLabel to all touchable elements without text
Description: Icon buttons and image-only Pressables have no accessible name without an explicit label
Do: Add accessibilityLabel to every Pressable TouchableOpacity Image without visible text; accessibilityHint for less obvious actions
Don't: Rely on image alt text or assume screen reader extracts label from children icons
Code Good: <Pressable accessibilityLabel='Add to cart' accessibilityRole='button' onPress={handleAdd}><Icon name='cart'/></Pressable>

Description: Role determines how screen reader announces and interacts with element; wrong role confuses users
Do: Use: button link text header image checkbox radio combobox; mark headings as header role; check for interactive elements
Don't: Default role='none' on Pressable (reads as 'button' anyway in some RN versions but inconsistent)
Code Bad: <Text style={styles.title}>Today's Tasks</Text> // No heading role announced

Guideline: Use FastImage for performant network images
Description: React Native's built-in Image component doesn't cache disk-to-memory efficiently; large lists with network images stutter
Do: Install react-native-fast-image; use priority system; resizeMode cover or contain; provide default placeholder
Don't: Use Image for large lists with network content; fetch same URL multiple times without caching
Code Good: <FastImage style={styles.avatar} source={{ uri: avatarUrl, priority: FastImage.priority.normal }} resizeMode={FastImage.resizeMode.cover}/>
Code Bad: <Image source={{ uri: avatarUrl }} style={styles.avatar} />  // No caching

Description: JS-thread animations (Animated API) block during heavy JS work; Reanimated runs on UI thread for smooth 60fps during JS busy periods
Do: Use reanimated2 useSharedValue useAnimatedStyle; keep animated values on UI thread; avoid runOnJS unless necessary
Don't: Use Animated.Value for complex gesture-driven animations; setValue in animation callbacks causing JS bounce
Code Good: const opacity = useSharedValue(0); const animStyle = useAnimatedStyle(()=>({ opacity: withSpring(opacity.value) })); useEffect(()=>{opacity.value=1},[])

Guideline: Always use useNativeDriver for Animated API
Description: useNativeDriver:true moves animation to native UI thread; false (default) runs on JS thread and jank occurs during JS processing
Do: Add useNativeDriver:true to every Animated timing/spring/decay; cannot use with layout properties (width height)
Don't: Forget useNativeDriver; use native driver with width/height/padding animations
Code Bad: Animated.timing(val, { toValue: 1, duration: 300 }).start() // defaults to JS thread

Guideline: Avoid passing setState down more than 2 levels
Description: Prop drilling setState through 3+ component levels creates coupling and makes refactoring painful
Do: Use React Context for 2-3 component depth state; use Zustand/Jotai for global state; colocate state close to where it's used
Don't: Thread setState through 5+ levels of props; use global state for local component state
Code Good: const CartContext = createContext(); export const useCart = () => useContext(CartContext); // Then wrap with <CartContext.Provider>

Guideline: Use React Native Testing Library for component tests
Description: Direct testing of RN component instances is brittle; RNTL provides semantic user-centric query methods
Do: Use screen.getByRole(); screen.getByLabelText(); fireEvent.press(); waitFor for async; test behavior not implementation
Don't: Query by component name or index; test internal state; mock entire children components when not necessary
Code Good: render(<LoginForm/>); const button = screen.getByRole('button', {name:'Sign In'}); fireEvent.press(button); await waitFor(()=>expect(screen.getByText('Welcome')).toBeTruthy())
Code Bad: const wrapper = shallow(<LoginForm/>); expect(wrapper.state('loading')).toBe(true)  // Enzyme testing internals

Guideline: Use Gesture Handler for complex touch interactions
Description: RN built-in touch events can't be easily composed or cancelled; gesture collisions and priority require Gesture Handler
Do: Use GestureDetector with Gesture.Pan().Tap().Simultaneous(); define gestureHandlerRootHOC at app root
Don't: Use PanResponder for complex gestures (callback hell); rely on onPress for multi-gesture interactions

Guideline: Wrap app root with proper providers
Description: Missing providers cause runtime errors hooks returning undefined or missing context; order matters
Do: GestureHandlerRootView → NavigationContainer → SafeAreaProvider → ThemeProvider → YourApp; GestureHandlerRootView must be outermost
Don't: Put NavigationContainer inside GestureHandlerRootView incorrectly; forget SafeAreaProvider; multiple NavigationContainers

Guideline: Handle network errors in data fetching gracefully
Description: Unhandled promise rejections and network errors cause silent failures or crashes; user sees blank screen
Do: Show error state UI; retry mechanism; network status awareness; use react-query or SWR for automatic retry
Don't: Ignore catch blocks; show nothing on network failure; crash on undefined data from failed fetch
Code Bad: fetch(url).then(res=>res.json()).then(setData) // No error handling - silent failure on network error
